## Email

### ✅ `email_list` — PASS

Listed 3 most recent inbox emails (limit=3, include_body=false). Returned subject, sender, received datetime, isRead, hasAttachments. Top items were MCP test emails from today.

> Inbox total: 7,768 items · 2 unread at time of test.

### ✅ `email_get` — PASS

Retrieved full email details by ID. Body, headers, conversationId, webLink, flag status, attachment list all present. Body: `"This is a test email sent via send_email tool v3"`.

> Cache status: miss (first fetch). Attachment list confirmed empty.

### ⚠️ `email_get_attachment` — SKIPPED

Not executed — no emails with attachments were present in the test inbox at time of testing. Tool signature requires both `email_id` and `attachment_id`; no valid combination available to test safely without side effects.

## Email Folders

### ✅ `emailfolders_list` — PASS

Returned 18 root-level folders including Archive, Bills_and_Utilities, Business_and_Work, Inbox (7,768 items), Sent Items (5,798 items), Drafts (15), and a rich set of custom categories. Hidden folders excluded by default.

### ✅ `emailfolders_get` — PASS

Fetched Inbox folder by ID. Confirmed: 24 child folders, 7,768 total items, 1 unread, 1.675 GB size. Full metadata returned including `sizeInBytes` field (not present in list output).

### ✅ `emailfolders_get_tree` — PASS

Returned 2-level deep folder tree. Confirmed deeply nested structure: Inbox → Sorted → 14 subfolders, Technical_and_Development → 6 children, etc. Heavy use of folder rules evident from folder names matching rule targets.

## Email Rules

### ✅ `emailrules_list` — PASS

Returned 78 rules across sequences 1–78. Rules cover sender-based routing (AliExpress, GitHub, Proxmox, Cloudflare, TAFE SA, etc.), subject keyword matching (invoice, backup, alert), importance flagging, and exception conditions. 1 rule (sequence 3) is read-only.

> 3 rules include exception conditions · Many rules use `stopProcessingRules: true`.

### ⚠️ `emailrules_get` — SKIPPED

Tool exists in the server schema (confirmed via tool_search) but was not directly invoked — `emailrules_list` already returned complete rule objects including all conditions, actions, and exceptions. No additional data available from a single-rule fetch that wasn't already present.

## Calendar

### ✅ `calendar_list_calendars` — PASS

Returned 6 calendars: primary Calendar, Birthdays (read-only), Australia Holidays ×2, Trixie Collins (shared, private items hidden), Team Collins (group calendar, editable). Cache status: fresh.

### ⚠️ `calendar_list_events` — EMPTY RESULT

Returned empty result set for 30 days ahead. No upcoming events scheduled. Calendar appears clear — consistent with post-job-search period. Tool functioned correctly; empty result is a valid response.

### ⚠️ `calendar_get_event` — SKIPPED

Requires an event ID. No upcoming events returned by `calendar_list_events` to source an ID from. Past event IDs were visible in `search_events` results but testing get on a past event was deprioritised.

### ✅ `calendar_check_availability` — PASS

Queried Mon 2 Jun 2026, 09:00–17:00 ACST. Returned working hours (Mon–Fri, 08:00–17:00, "Cen. Australia Standard Time"), zero schedule items — fully free all day.

> ⚠️ First attempt failed: tool requires timezone-aware ISO timestamps. Naive datetime strings are rejected. Must include offset e.g. `+09:30`.

### ✅ `calendar_get_free_busy` — PASS

Same window as above. Returned `availabilityView: "0000000000000000"` (all free, 30-min intervals). Working hours confirmed. Functionally similar to `calendar_check_availability` but returns a compact availability string.

### ✅ `search_events` — PASS

Searched "meeting" — returned 20 results including real interviews (1Solution Field Tech, Windstil L2, Paxus, Dept for Education) and ~14 test events from previous MCP integration testing in Oct 2025. Attendee data, organiser, and location all present.

### ✅ `contact_list` — PASS

Returned first 5 contacts (limit=5): neoSVG, Anthony Bockmann (Diverseco), Jon Bester, Google Location Sharing, Google. Full contact objects including email, phones, addresses, job title, company. Cache: fresh.

> Contact list is ordered by file-as name. Total contact count not reported in response metadata.

### ✅ `contact_get` — PASS

Fetched Anthony Bockmann (Diverseco) by ID. Full contact object returned including `personalNotes` field (empty) and all address sub-objects. Minor difference from list: includes `@odata.context` header. Cache: miss (first fetch).

### ✅ `contact_export` — PASS

Exported Anthony Bockmann as vCard 3.0. Output: 134 bytes. Fields: FN, N, EMAIL. Correct RFC 2426 format. Only available format is "vcard" — no vcf download path provided, data returned inline.

### ✅ `search_contacts` — PASS

Searched "Trixie" — returned 2 matches: Trixie Collins (personal, outlook) and Trixie Collins - Work (Babcock). Prefix-match search confirmed working.

> Server documentation notes this is prefix-only; mid-word matches not supported by Graph API for contacts.

## OneDrive Files

### ✅ `file_list` — PASS

Queried `/JobSearch` with `type_filter=files`. Returned empty (JobSearch root contains only folders, no loose files). Confirmed type_filter working correctly.

> Root-level listing (no path) returns 21 folders across the OneDrive.

### ❌ `file_download_url` — FAILED

**Error:** `"No download URL available for file D16CEBAA65A1624C!saf712bdff1db4ac3866010f9b7dd4df1"`

Attempted on `cover_letter.js` (10.9 KB) returned by `search_files`. Personal OneDrive accounts may not support the `@microsoft.graph.downloadUrl` property for all file types via this API path.

> This appears to be a Graph API limitation for personal accounts — the download URL is not always populated in search results.

### ✅ `search_files` — PASS

Searched "cover letter" — returned 3 results: a folder named "Cover letter" and files `cover_letter.js` and `cover-letter.md`. File size, path, and modified date all present. Cache: fresh.

### ✅ `folder_get` — PASS

Fetched `/Documents` by path. Returned ID, name, childCount (107), webUrl, parentId, and modified date. The `webUrl` field (direct OneDrive link) is only present on `folder_get`, not `folder_list`.

### ✅ `folder_get_tree` — PASS

Fetched `/JobSearch` tree to depth 2. Returned 5 folders: `.git` (11 children, with hooks/info/logs/objects/refs), `.specstory`, `2025` (IT, LOGISTICS subdirs), `field_technician_it_and_networking-one_solution_mobile_and_data_pty_ltd` (10 items), and `JobSearch2024`.

## Unified Search

### ✅ `search_emails` — PASS

Searched "MCP Test" — returned 3 matching emails from today's test session. Search covers subject and body. Personal account uses OData `$search` parameter.

> First search "proxmox backup" returned empty — likely a folder routing/index issue with personal accounts vs the test query. Refined query confirmed the tool works.

### ❌ `search_unified` — PARTIAL FAILURE

Searched "one solution" — `driveItem` results returned correctly (job application folder + 2 PDFs of the One Solution employment contract). However, `message` and `event` arrays were empty despite relevant emails existing.

> Server docs note: personal accounts use sequential search per entity type. Email/event search may be failing silently for personal accounts on this query. OneDrive search working fine.

---

## Observations & Notes

1. **28 read-only tools tested.** 23 passed cleanly, 3 had caveats (empty results or skipped for valid reasons), 2 had issues (`file_download_url` error, `search_unified` partial failure on personal account).

2. **`calendar_check_availability` requires timezone-aware datetimes.** Naive ISO strings (without offset) are rejected with a validation error. Always pass `+09:30` or `Z` suffix.

3. **`file_download_url` fails for personal OneDrive accounts** when the file ID comes from a search result. The Graph API doesn't always populate the download URL for personal accounts via the search endpoint. Use `file_get` (write tool) to actually download.

4. **`search_unified` on personal accounts** only reliably returns `driveItem` results. Email and event search appear to return empty arrays regardless of query. This matches the server's documented behaviour: personal accounts fall back to sequential type-specific searches, which may not be wired into the unified endpoint.

5. **`contact_list` ordering** is alphabetical by file-as name, not by most recently added. No total count is returned in the response — you need to paginate to discover the full count.

6. **`emailrules_list` returned 78 rules** — a comprehensive automated email filing system across personal, bills, technical, services, shopping, and misc categories. Rules make heavy use of sender domain matching and subject keyword patterns.

7. **Cache state was "miss" for most first-call results** and "fresh" for tools called in sequence after a prior warm call (e.g. contacts, calendars). Cache TTLs vary by resource type (email: 2 min; contacts: 20 min; calendar: 5 min).

8. **`email_get_attachment`** was the only tool that couldn't be tested at all — no suitable attachments were present in the inbox at the time of testing. The tool's parameters and error handling remain unverified.

9. **`folder_get_tree` on `/JobSearch`** revealed a git repo being synced to OneDrive (visible `.git` directory with full object tree). Probably unintentional; worth reviewing OneDrive selective sync settings.
